mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound (below 0.5%) signals persistently accommodative monetary policy, lowering the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or returns during sub-0.5% FEDFUNDS periods are not significantly higher than the full-sample mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals a deteriorating labor market that leads investors to reprice future corporate earnings downward, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the average triggered-period return is not statistically distinguishable from non-triggered periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Positive co-movement between retail sales growth (RSAFS) and payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) indicates a self-reinforcing consumption-income cycle; when both accelerate simultaneously, forward S&P 500 returns over the next month are positively affected, but neither series alone reliably predicts returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 2% AND PAYEMS 3-month change > 0
failure_condition: The joint-trigger signal shows Rank IC no higher than either single-series signal (RSAFS-only or PAYEMS-only) over a 3-year rolling window, indicating no true interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of industrial production expansion (INDPRO year-over-year growth positive), continued growth in INDPRO signals healthy corporate output and profit momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter; this relationship is expected to be much weaker or reversed during industrial contractions.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns conditional on positive INDPRO YoY growth are not significantly different from returns conditional on negative INDPRO YoY growth (p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in housing starts exceeding 8% signals an abrupt weakening in interest-rate-sensitive demand, foreshadowing risk-off sentiment that produces negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following triggered HOUST declines are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the average triggered return is statistically indistinguishable from unconditional 5-day returns (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is near zero and headline CPI growth simultaneously accelerates above 2% year-over-year, the combination signals a policy lag risk in which markets anticipate future tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month; neither variable alone captures this repricing.
failure_condition: The joint FEDFUNDS-CPIAUCSL trigger signal underperforms a naive CPIAUCSL-only threshold signal in out-of-sample Rank IC, or triggered-period returns are not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payroll employment reflects ongoing labor market strength that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, producing modestly positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing PAYEMS growth and 5-day forward returns remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime where real GDP (GDPC1) growth has decelerated to below 2% annualized for two consecutive quarters, continued deceleration signals rising recession risk that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter; in early or mid-cycle regimes with GDPC1 growth above 2%, the same deceleration signal has no reliable directional effect.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 2% for two consecutive quarters
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following the triggered GDPC1 deceleration are not significantly more negative than unconditional 60-day returns (p > 0.05), or the effect reverses sign consistently across two or more late-cycle episodes.
